learning_objectives: Learn Accessible Emission Limits (AELs) for each class
Understand classification rules for complex sources

standard_info.title: Safety of laser products – Part 1: Equipment classification and requirements

scope.exclusions: Lasers emitting below 180 nm (vacuum UV) as they require evacuated enclosures
applications_covered: Demonstration of physical and optical phenomena

key_concepts: Accessible Emission Limit (AEL) - maximum permitted emission for a class
Classification based on AE compared to AEL

sheets.committee: IEC TC 76: Optical radiation safety and laser equipment

important_notes: Clarifications apply equivalently to MPE analysis in Annex A
Colour inside logo indicates color printing is recommended for understanding

content_pages.title: Classification Rules - Multiple Wavelengths and Extended Sources

default_method.exception: 400-600 nm for durations > 100s - angle not limited

extended_sources.class_3b_evaluation: Angle of acceptance is not limited

non_uniform_sources.rule: Vary angle of acceptance in each dimension
non_uniform_sources.general_principle: Same emission duration t used for AEL determination and αmax calculation

quiz_questions.question: For extended sources in the wavelength range 400-1400 nm using default evaluation, what is the standard angle of acceptance limit?
quiz_questions.explanation: When using the default (simplified) evaluation method for wavelengths ≥ 400 nm and < 1,400 nm, the angle of acceptance may be limited to 100 mrad.

content.example_description: Analysis of 20 emitters with different groupings
content.conclusion: Vertical two-element group has maximum AE/AEL ratio (1.2), meaning Class 2 AEL is exceeded by factor of 1.2

pulse_groups.condition: α > 5 mrad and group duration > Ti

key_parameters.T2: Calculated with αmax limited to 100 mrad

content.thermal_grouping: When individual pulses are close together, they represent one 'effective' pulse

content_pages.title: Pulse Duration Analysis and Critical Period

pulse_group_analysis.integration_window: Must vary in temporal position and duration
pulse_group_analysis.simplified_analysis: Pulses with sufficient temporal spacing need not be considered as groups

critical_period.formula_alpha_greater: Tcrit = 0.01 × αmax × tpulse^0.5 (tpulse in seconds, αmax in mrad)
critical_period.note: αmax not limited to αmax value

conditions_for_grouping: Pulse group durations between Ti and 0.25s

larger_apparent_source.issue: AEL can be more restrictive for larger α values (contrary to biophysical principles)
larger_apparent_source.solution: Classification may be based on α < 5 mrad even if actual source is larger

square_aperture.rule: Square aperture stop (7 mm side) equivalent to or more restrictive than circular

simplified_analysis.applicability: Still valid even if actual α > 5 mrad

moving_sources.description: Scanned emission not accommodating to pivot point
moving_sources.c5_determination: Use stationary apparent source α for inequalities

totp_method.definition: Sum of all pulse durations within emission duration or T2
totp_method.pulse_assignment: Pulses < Ti assigned duration of Ti

limitations.reason: Small peak power pulses might increase AEL more than total energy

varying_peak_power.method: Count pulses based on relative peak power
varying_peak_power.threshold: Don't count pulses < 10% of maximum peak power

quiz_questions.question: What does TOTP stand for in laser classification?
quiz_questions.explanation: TOTP stands for Total-On-Time Pulse, which is the sum of all pulse durations within the emission duration or T2.

radiance_criterion.description: Criterion based on radiance for laser products functioning as conventional lamps

content.measurement_distance: 200 mm from closest point of human access

inhomogeneous_sources.rule: Use outer edge (50% level) of apparent source profile
inhomogeneous_sources.application: Both radiance limit and minimum source size

radiance_limit_lt.nature: Not an AEL, merely a criterion
radiance_limit_lt.does_not_imply: Product is 'safe' or specific Risk Group under IEC 62471

classification_requirements.applicable: Requirements of IEC 60825-1 still apply (labels, engineering, service, user info)
classification_requirements.class_1_label: Mandatory for products with emission excluded under 4.4
classification_requirements.additional_label: Risk Group label according to IEC 62471 series

component_modules.light_modules: Not subject to IEC 60825-1 when sold as components
component_modules.end_products: Luminaires are in scope including subclause 4.4

quiz_questions.question: For a Class 1 laser product with emission excluded under subclause 4.4, is the Class 1 label mandatory or optional?
quiz_questions.explanation: Contrary to normal Class 1 products where the label is optional, for products with light emission excluded under 4.4, the Class 1 label is mandatory.

document_structure: 5. Determination of accessible emission level and classification
6. Engineering specifications (access panels, interlocks, remote interlock)
9. Additional requirements for specific laser products
annexes: Annex A: Maximum Permissible Exposure (MPE) tables
Annex E: MPEs and AELs as radiance
Annex G: Overview of associated parts

specific_warnings: Skin and anterior eye hazard warning

content.measurement_condition_1: Accessible emission at closest point of human access
content.measurement_condition_2: At specified distance with specified aperture
content.measurement_condition_3: For collimated beams at specified reference points

extended_source_evaluation.method_1: Image apparent source onto field stop plane
extended_source_evaluation.method_2: Place circular aperture close to apparent source

accessible_emission_limits.description: Safe except when viewed with magnifying optics
accessible_emission_limits.warning: Do not view directly with optical instruments

accessible_emission_limits.description: Visible lasers, eye protection by blink reflex
As Class 2 but unsafe with magnifying optics
Low risk, normally safe with aversion response
High power, fire and diffuse reflection hazard

glossary.AE: Accessible Emission - measured radiation level at specified conditions
glossary.AEL: Accessible Emission Limit - maximum permitted emission for a laser class
glossary.MPE: Maximum Permissible Exposure - safe exposure limit for humans
glossary.αmax: Maximum angular subtense of apparent source
glossary.T2: Time base for thermal hazard assessment

related_standards: IEC 62471: Photobiological safety of lamps and lamp systems
